name: Automate Cleanup of Stale Merged Branches
description: Remove local and remote branches that have already been merged, keeping your repository clean and navigable.
trendingReason: Teams adopting trunk-based development need automated branch hygiene to manage high-frequency merges.
prerequisites: A repository with multiple merged branches

steps.title: Fetch and prune remote tracking branches
steps.description: Sync with the remote and remove local references to branches that no longer exist on the remote.
steps.tip: Run this regularly or add it to your git config with `git config --global fetch.prune true` to auto-prune on every fetch.

steps.title: List all merged local branches
steps.description: Show local branches that have been fully merged into main. The grep excludes main, master, and the current branch.
steps.warning: Always verify the list before deleting. Some long-lived branches like release or develop may appear as merged.

steps.title: Delete merged local branches
steps.command: git branch --merged main | grep -v 'main\|master\|\*' | xargs -r git branch -d
steps.description: Pipe the merged branch list into xargs to delete each one. The -d flag only deletes fully merged branches, so it is safe.
steps.tip: The -r flag on xargs prevents an error when there are no branches to delete.

steps.title: List merged remote branches
steps.description: Show remote tracking branches that have been merged into main, stripping the origin/ prefix for readability.

steps.title: Delete merged remote branches
steps.command: git branch -r --merged main | grep -v 'main\|master\|HEAD' | sed 's/origin\///' | xargs -r -I {} git push origin --delete {}
steps.description: Delete each merged remote branch. The -I {} placeholder passes each branch name to the push --delete command.
steps.warning: This is irreversible on the remote. Make sure no one else is working on these branches before deleting.

steps.title: Verify cleanup results
steps.description: List all remaining local and remote branches to confirm the stale ones have been removed.

fullScript:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# branch-cleanup.sh — Remove merged local and remote branches
set -euo pipefail

BASE_BRANCH="${1:-main}"

echo "Fetching and pruning remote references..."
git fetch --prune

echo ""
echo "=== Merged local branches ==="
MERGED_LOCAL=$(git branch --merged "$BASE_BRANCH" | grep -v "main\|master\|\*" || true)
if [ -z "$MERGED_LOCAL" ]; then
  echo "No merged local branches to clean up."
else
  echo "$MERGED_LOCAL"
  echo ""
  read -rp "Delete these local branches? (y/N) " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    echo "$MERGED_LOCAL" | xargs -r git branch -d
    echo "Local branches deleted."
  fi
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Merged remote branches ==="
MERGED_REMOTE=$(git branch -r --merged "$BASE_BRANCH" | grep -v "main\|master\|HEAD" | sed 's/origin\///' || true)
if [ -z "$MERGED_REMOTE" ]; then
  echo "No merged remote branches to clean up."
else
  echo "$MERGED_REMOTE"
  echo ""
  read -rp "Delete these remote branches? (y/N) " confirm
  if [[ "$confirm" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    echo "$MERGED_REMOTE" | xargs -r -I {} git push origin --delete {}
    echo "Remote branches deleted."
  fi
fi

echo ""
echo "Cleanup complete. Remaining branches:"
git branch -a

faq.question: Will this delete branches that are open in a pull request?
faq.answer: It only deletes branches merged into the base branch. If a PR is open but not yet merged, its branch will not appear in the --merged list and will be safe.

faq.question: How can I protect specific branches from being deleted?
faq.answer: Add them to the grep exclusion pattern. For example, add '\|develop\|release' to the grep -v filter to protect those branches.

faq.question: Can I schedule this to run automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. Add the script as a cron job, a CI/CD scheduled pipeline, or a GitHub Action that runs weekly. Use the non-interactive version by removing the read prompts and adding a --force flag.
